task.title: Installing DITA-OT
task.shortdesc: Download, install, and verify DITA Open Toolkit 4.x on your machine so you can build DITA content into HTML5, PDF, and other output formats.

prereq.p: Before you begin, ensure you have:

ul.li: Java 17 or later — Check with java -version. If not installed, download from adoptium.net.
500 MB free disk space for the toolkit and plugins
Network access to download the distribution

context.p: DITA-OT (DITA Open Toolkit) is the open-source publishing engine for DITA content. It transforms DITA XML sources into HTML5, PDF, EPUB, and other output formats.

step.cmd: Download the latest release from the DITA-OT website.

info.p: Go to dita-ot.org/download and download the .zip distribution.

step.stepresult: You have a file named dita-ot-4.2.zip (or similar) in your downloads folder.

step.cmd: Unzip the distribution to your preferred directory.
step.stepresult: A dita-ot-4.2/ directory is created containing the toolkit files.

step.cmd: Add the bin/ directory to your system PATH.

info.p: Add this line to your shell profile (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, etc.):

step.cmd: Verify the installation by checking the version.
step.stepresult: You should see output like: DITA-OT version 4.2

result.p: DITA-OT is installed and ready to use. You can now build DITA content with commands like:

ul.li: Set up your XML editor (Oxygen XML, VS Code with DITA extensions, or similar)
Build a sample project from the dita-ot-4.2/docsrc/ directory to test your setup
Install custom plugins using dita install <plugin-url>
